clinical_history: What AV conduction abnormality is present in this ECG showing  sinus rhythm with right bundle branch block and left atrial abnormality?
diagnosis: c) The record begins with a long AV Wenckebach (type I block) sequence with 6:5 block, followed by 2:1 block, and then the onset of a longer sequence of progressively prolonging PR intervals. This pattern is characteristic of AV Wenckebach and not Mobitz II AV block or complete heart block. Note that some of the P waves are partly "hidden" in the T waves. The PR after the non-conducted P wave is considerably shorter than the one before, a hallmark of AV Wenckebach. The fact that the sinus P waves come "on time" excludes blocked premature atrial depolarizations. For an example of isorhythmic AV dissociation, please see Case # 243.
